title: Statistical Mechanics and Symmetries of Non-Abelian Anyon Proliferation: From Deformation to Decoherence
authors: Avi Vadali, Robijn Vanhove, Ruben Verresen, Jason Alicea, Pablo Sala
abstract: Topological quantum computation relies on braiding non-Abelian anyons, but requires the underlying topological order to survive imperfect state preparation and environmental noise. We show that the instability of topological order to wavefunction deformations and to decoherence, with the latter probed by syndrome distributions, are generically captured by stat-mech models whose symmetries naturally expose the corrupting anyonic excitations. As an example, we combine this framework with Monte-Carlo simulations to resolve the stability of $D_4$ topological order under deformations and quantum channels that proliferate multiple non-Abelian anyon species that individually are unable to condense. We show that beyond a finite threshold, proliferation of two non-Abelian anyon species parasitically condenses a shared Abelian-anyon fusion outcome, destroying the topological order. Our symmetry-based approach sharply differentiates the resulting trivial phase from that obtained by condensing all Abelian charges; in other words, the trivial phase "remembers" which anyons condensed. This framework provides a first step into identifying the relevant symmetry for optimal decoders, conditioned on syndrome measurements, of non-Abelian topological order.
summary: This work develops a statistical mechanics approach to study how non-Abelian topological order breaks down under decoherence. By modeling the system using coupled loop models and analyzing anyon condensation, the authors determine critical error thresholds and characterize the symmetry structure of the resulting trivial phases.
main_problem: The study investigates how topological order, maintained by non-Abelian anyons, degrades due to environmental noise (decoherence) or wavefunction deformations.
main_result: The proliferation of multiple non-Abelian anyon species can drive the system into a trivial phase below a finite error threshold, and the resulting symmetry structure is characterized by $G 	imes 	ext{Rep}(G)$.
method: The authors employ statistical mechanics models derived from quantum double structures, utilizing Monte Carlo simulations, Binder cumulants, and analyzing anyon syndrome distributions.
model_or_system: The system models topological order based on non-Abelian anyons (e.g., in $D_4$ TO), analyzed through coupled loop models, spin Hamiltonians, and the resulting statistical mechanics partition functions.
key_observables: Binder cumulants ($U$), correlation functions of Wilson operators ($\langle n_R^b \cdot n_R^g angle$), purity ($	ext{tr}(ho^2)$), and anyon syndrome distributions $p(\{a_v\})$.
important_parameters: Decoherence rates ($p_\alpha$), deformation parameters ($ eta_R,  eta_B$), and the critical error threshold $p_c(\infty)$.
main_assumptions: The analysis often relies on mapping complex quantum dynamics onto effective classical statistical mechanics models (e.g., loop/string-net models) and assuming specific symmetry structures.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate lattice structures, phase diagrams showing condensation thresholds for multiple anyon species ($mR, mB, eG$), and plots of Binder cumulants diagnosing transitions.
paper_structure: The paper systematically develops the statistical mechanics description by deriving loop models from quantum double algebras, analyzing stability under noise via syndrome distributions, and mapping out phase diagrams based on condensation thresholds for different anyon species.
why_interesting: It provides a powerful framework connecting abstract topological order concepts to measurable thermodynamic quantities (like susceptibility or cumulants) in the presence of realistic environmental noise.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Coupled loop model and fusion processes. (a) Lattice for the stat-mech model in Eq. (2), with r, g, b respectively labeling centers of red, green, blue hexagons. Shown loop configuration has cyclomatic number CLR∪LB = 1 + 1 + 3 = 5. (b) Allowed fusions between mR and mB anyons. From left to right: mB × mB →1; mR × mR →1; and, (mB × mB) × (mR × mR) →1 × 1 = 1, or (mB × mB) × (mR × mR) →eG × eG = 1.

FIG. 2. Deformation and quantum channel simula- tions. (a) Phase diagram capturing mR, mB, eG condensa- tion with the associated GmR,mB →Z2 2 classical transition. Red line corresponds to data from panels (b,c), blue and green lines indicate traces from App. D, and tTC = 1/ √

FIG. 4. Boltzmann weights of the coupled-loop stat- mech model. Loops interact by exchanging a trivial charge and an Abelian charge (eG for the red/blue interaction) (a) and in addition braid non-trivially when they intersect (c). (b,d) A non-interacting loop segment acts trivially in its re- spective 2-dimensional space. Other weights are analogously defined.

FIG. 3. MPO anyon network. One particular anyon con- figuration created by a deformation on a plaquette of the D4 quantum double groundstate: an mR and mB-anyon pair is created in the specified order. The overlap forces the final anyon on every plaquette to be the vacuum, indicated by the projector P 1, and evaluates to a closed network of fusion ten- sors, representing the braiding and fusion processes consistent with the given ordering. The network is exactly a string-net strange correlator as in Eq. (12).
